senses.definition: in relation to a region or regions; with respect to a particular region
examples: Regionally, the country shows different dialects and customs.
The product is marketed regionally, not nationwide.

senses.definition: people or things from a particular region; regional sports or competitions
examples: The team won the regionals and advanced to the national finals.
Regionals often feature local teams and talents.

senses.definition: relating to a region or regions; regional in scope
examples: regionary maps show distribution by area.
Regionary laws apply within specified districts.

senses.definition: past participle of region; not commonly used as a stand-alone verb; may appear in specialized contexts meaning 'to divide into regions'
examples: The country was regioned into administrative zones.
The map regioned the territory for planning.

senses.definition: areas or parts of a country, city, or the world that share common features
examples: The Amazon region is rich in biodiversity.
Students come from different regions of the country.

senses.definition: to gird again; to encircle or surround again
examples: They regird the wound with fresh bandages.

senses.definition: a person who supervises or directs the preparation of a performance or production; director
examples: The regisseur coordinated the stage design with the actors.
In some theatres, the regisseur handles rehearsal planning.

senses.definition: plural of regisseur; multiple people who supervise or direct productions
examples: The regisseurs met to discuss the tour.
Two regisseurs shared responsibilities for the festival.

senses.definition: an official list or record; a device for recording information
examples: Please sign the register at the front desk.
The register of births is kept by the city.

senses.definition: the level of formality in language or writing
examples: The speech was written in a formal register.
Casual register is fine in emails to friends.

senses.definition: to sign up or enroll for something; to record information officially
examples: She registered for the course last week.
Workers must register their devices with IT.

senses.definition: to record or enroll to receive something
examples: Registrations are open until Friday.
He registered to vote in the new district.

senses.definition: capable of being registered or recorded
examples: The invention is registerable as a patent.
Only registerable items can be entered in the system.

senses.definition: capable of being registered; eligible for registration
examples: This mark is registrable in multiple jurisdictions.
Not all products are registrable under the new policy.

senses.definition: relating to or concerning registers or registry entries
examples: Registral data showed the ownership history.
The registral system tracks changes over time.

senses.definition: a person or organization that registers or is registered
examples: All registrants will receive a confirmation email.
The registrant must provide identification.

senses.definition: plural of registrant; people or organizations that have registered
examples: The conference announced the names of the registrants.
Registrants come from many countries.

senses.definition: an official or organization responsible for maintaining a register or registry
examples: The registrar keeps birth records.
He works as a registrar at the university.

senses.definition: plural of registrary; an office or person responsible for records
examples: The registraries handle all recordkeeping tasks.
Different registraries may exist for birth and marriage records.

senses.definition: plural of registrar; officials who maintain official registers
examples: Registrars verify documents for citizenship.
Several registrars attended the conference.

senses.definition: the office or position of a registrar; the duties of keeping official records
examples: Her registrarship involves managing student records.
The registrar's ship handles vital statistics.
